title: After-Action Report — /new-league FK Crash + Unrecoverable DB
scope: The session was originally scoped to monitoring /api/health. The health logs themselves looked fine — 200 on every probe — but the same log stream surfaced a real-user crash on /new-league followed by every subsequent request 500'ing on a different error. That's what the user was actually stuck on.

rationale.item: "still not able to get the league started again after the last 2 PRs"
"i try to start a new league in the pro/rel universe and it crashes and then i can't get it back at all."

Three tables owned by o27v2/auction.py (auction_keepers, auction_results, auction_lot_bids) REFERENCES teams(id)/players(id) but were missing from drop_all()'s drop list; once they hold rows, DROP TABLE players/teams raises FOREIGN KEY constraint failed and drop_all() aborts halfway, killing the app.

executescript runs each DROP TABLE in sequence and SQLite commits each one as it goes; when the script raised midway, tables already dropped (including sim_meta) stayed dropped. Every page-load context processor calls get_current_sim_date(), so the whole app went down.

actions.description: Added the three auction tables explicitly to drop_all()'s drop list, first since they're pure children of teams/players.
Wrapped the whole drop script in PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF/ON with try/finally so drop order doesn't matter and any future referencing table is structurally safe.
Wrote a focused integration repro before fixing — init_db + init_auction_schema + inserted teams/players/auction_keepers rows, then drop_all() (lives in the AAR, not committed).

risk_posture: The `try/finally` makes sure FKs are restored even if some other exception (corrupt DB, locked file) escapes the script — the connection is short-lived but the PRAGMA flips to a session-scoped default at SQLite level, and being defensive here is cheap.
Skipping for now to keep the fix minimal.
Total diff: 26 insertions, 17 deletions in 1 file.

validation_claims.claim: Before the fix: raises sqlite3.IntegrityError: FOREIGN KEY constraint failed exactly as in production.
validation_claims.check_method: Run the repro snippet from the AAR against the pre-fix drop_all

validation_claims.claim: After the fix: drop_all OK; remaining tables ['season_batting_leaders', 'season_pitching_leaders', 'season_standings', 'seasons', 'sqlite_sequence']; full table set restored after reseed; FK pragma still ON: 1.
validation_claims.check_method: Run the repro snippet against the fixed drop_all and inspect sqlite_master and PRAGMA foreign_keys

validation_claims.claim: The repro test verified this: after drop_all(), those four [seasons-archive] tables are still present.
validation_claims.check_method: Run drop_all on a seeded DB and list remaining tables

non_actions.item: No matching test in the repo's tests/ directory; the repro lives in the AAR rather than as a committed regression test
non_actions.reason: seeding a two-league DB without dragging in the rest of seed_league requires either fixture infra that doesn't exist yet or a brittle hand-built state. The structural fix (FKs off + the auction tables in the drop list) is the thing that prevents the class of bug. Skipping for now to keep the fix minimal.

non_actions.item: Seasons-archive tables (seasons, season_standings, season_batting_leaders, season_pitching_leaders) deliberately not dropped
non_actions.reason: matching the existing comment in the schema: Task #62 archived season history persists ACROSS the drop_all() / reseed cycle so a multi-season test run can compare model output across seasons.

non_actions.item: No special migration for the user's broken half-dropped DB
non_actions.reason: On the next request init_db() runs its executescript(SCHEMA) step, which is CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for every table — missing tables come back, surviving rows in untouched tables stay.

residual_risks: It's a regression that a fresh-DB integration test wouldn't catch — it requires DB state from a prior season.
open_hooks: If we want a guard test later, the right shape is: test_drop_all_with_full_referencing_state — seed_minimal_league_with_auction_run(); db.drop_all(); no raise.
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — /new-league FK Crash + Unrecoverable DB
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/monitor-api-health-ypTDM; Commit: dec35cf. | Error traces: POST /new-league raised sqlite3.IntegrityError: FOREIGN KEY constraint failed at o27v2/db.py:775 drop_all; subsequent requests raised sqlite3.OperationalError: no such table: sim_meta at o27v2/sim.py:1707 get_current_sim_date. | drop_all()'s known drop list and the auction.py table definitions (lines 46-80) quoted in full. | PR archaeology: the "last 2 PRs" were #36 (Live auction polish: render fix on FINAL PRICE + flag emojis + /youth schema) and #37 (Fresh-league 500: derive_linear_weights default for missing event types); neither touched drop_all(), but #37 was the first time most users actually got to the auction stage on a fresh league — exposing the latent FK gap that #35 (new-league-structure) had opened. | Bug only triggers when a previous league ran init_auction_schema, the auction ran (rows inserted), and the user starts a second league. | Files touched: o27v2/db.py:768 — drop_all(): added auction tables; wrapped executescript in PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF/ON with try/finally.
parser_notes: Clean bug-fix AAR; repro code lives in the document rather than the test suite (explicitly noted).
